world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Green Row appears peaceful with populated town, functioning shops, royal governance intact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs conduct normal activities; townspeople available for dialogue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Missing townspeople absence in Green Row; dungeon fully exposed and accessible; magical corruption evident
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King requests aid; remaining NPCs express concern; enemies active in dungeon

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dungeon reveals wizard guild and goblin settlements; kidnapped people scattered across levels; dark magic effects visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies engage in combat; magical hazards present; Roke conducting sacrifice ritual

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ramiah's seal weakens visually; magical intensity increases in dungeon depths; boss encounter arena activates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Roke performs final sacrifice; Ramiah enters combat state; ultimate confrontation triggered

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dungeon cleared; demon defeated; Green Row restored to normalcy
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Townspeople thank David; royal family expresses gratitude; David departs peacefully

environmental_storytelling.example: Safe above-ground Green Row with castle/shops versus dangerous multi-level dungeon hidden beneath graveyard communicates power hierarchy and moral geography
Dungeon contains wizard guild and goblin town, revealing underground civilization and political complexity beyond surface kingdom
Freeing kidnapped townspeople from dungeon and returning them to Green Row visually tracks protagonist progress and world-state restoration
Simultaneous display of David's and boss enemy's health creates visual parity and dramatic tension during crucial confrontations
Hidden stairwell in cemetery establishes supernatural threshold between safe town and dangerous underworld
Four classical elements (air, fire, water, earth) accessible to player but also used by enemies/wizards; shows magical saturation of world
Inventory, map (player-filled), and magic switching indicate player agency and knowledge accumulation about world state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Running, jumping, sword attacks; magic switching via pause menu; free roaming in safe zones
Sword attacks at standstill or while moving; mid-air lunging; elemental magic deployment; combined magic usage
Health bar visibly depleting; limited offensive capability; dependency on healing items
Stationary or patrol patterns in dungeon areas
Direct combat engagement; attacking player; pursuing player through levels
Dedicated boss arena battle; separate health bar display; advanced combat patterns
Conducting kidnapping operations; preparing demon sacrifice ritual; maintaining magical control of dungeon
Performs self-sacrifice to resurrect Ramiah; triggers ultimate threat
Contained within dungeon depths; dormant threat
Boss combat engagement; final obstacle requiring defeat to restore world peace
Scattered throughout dungeon levels; require rescue; unable to contribute to town life
Grateful dialogue; restoration to town locations; narrative closure signaling
Requests aid from visiting knight; expresses concern; initiates quest narrative
Thanks David publicly; reinstates normal governance; world-state returns to peace

notes: Light Crusader uses isometric perspective to create visual depth for dungeon exploration. The game communicates political legitimacy through the monarchy's castle architecture and the king's role as quest-giver. Danger is primarily signaled through dungeon presence, enemy encounters, and the darkly motivated antagonist (Roke's rejection-driven revenge). Safety is reinforced through the pastoral town environment with shops and NPCs. The dual health bar system creates visual parity between player and boss, enhancing dramatic tension. Magic system (four classical elements with combination mechanics) serves as both gameplay mechanic and world-state indicator, showing the magical saturation of the setting. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on location-based narrative (above-ground kingdom vs. underground dungeon civilization) and the progression of rescued townspeople returning to Green Row.
